title: Earnings Call Transcript: EVgo Q1 2026 Results Show Revenue Growth, EPS Miss
text: EVgo reported Q1 2026 earnings results with record revenue of $110 million, marking a 45% year-over-year increase. The company reported an earnings per share of -$0.12, slightly missing the forecasted -$0.11. The company added more than 200 new direct current fast charging stalls in Q1 and now has 5,280 stalls in operation. For 2026, EVgo expects the second quarter to be the weakest quarter in terms of revenue and margins, with management guiding second-quarter 2026 revenue of $75 million to $85 million, below visible alpha consensus of $106.1 million. Guidance for 2026 is reaffirmed with strong liquidity from amended DOE and commercial loans supporting aggressive stall deployment and long-term growth. EVgo announced it revised its loan with the Department of Energy to a maximum guaranteed loan amount of $750 million, down from approximately $1.248 billion. This led to analyst adjustments including Cantor Fitzgerald adjusting its price target to $4.00 from $6.00 while maintaining an Overweight rating.
